top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 103, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
83ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Levi's GranFondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
Levi's GranFondo's paved Sonoma County roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 103 miles.
At Levi's GranFondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Levi's GranFondo. Train it until it feels boring.
8,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,500ft of climbing on paved Sonoma County roads does to your power file on race day.
8,500ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Levi's GranFondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 103 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
103 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Levi's GranFondo’s 103 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Levi's GranFondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved sonoma county roads to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 103 miles of paved sonoma county roads in Santa Rosa, Sonoma County, California, USA.
